message:
feat: persist tmux styling + Claude /rename across session respawns

tmux session styling (status bar color, pane borders) and Claude Code's
/rename label are both per-session. When the supervisor kills and
respawns the session (on crash, renew timer, or healthcheck stall),
both vanish — the operator has to manually re-apply them every time.

Supervisor now re-applies both on every start_session, reading from
four optional env vars:

- AGENT_TMUX_STATUS_STYLE          — full tmux status-style value
- AGENT_TMUX_PANE_BORDER_STYLE     — pane-border-style
- AGENT_TMUX_PANE_ACTIVE_BORDER_STYLE — pane-active-border-style
- AGENT_CLAUDE_RENAME_TO           — label for Claude Code /rename

agent.env.example documents each with example color codes and notes
that /rename is sent AFTER the /loop prompt has had a few seconds to
land (so /rename doesn't get absorbed as part of the loop body).

Leave any of the vars unset to skip — existing deployments that
don't set them are unaffected.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -39,6 +39,16 @@ NOTIFY_ON_PHRASE_REGEX="${AGENT_NOTIFY_ON_PHRASE_REGEX:-}"
 NOTIFY_ON_PHRASE_TITLE="${AGENT_NOTIFY_ON_PHRASE_TITLE:-Agent needs attention}"
 NOTIFY_ON_PHRASE_BODY="${AGENT_NOTIFY_ON_PHRASE_BODY:-Matched notify phrase in session $SESSION on $(hostname). Check the pane and take whatever action you prefer; the supervisor will not act on its own.}"
 
+# Display persistence across respawns. tmux session styling (status bar
+# color, pane border color) is per-session and disappears when the session
+# is killed. Claude Code's /rename slash command also disappears. These env
+# vars let the supervisor re-apply both every time it spawns the session.
+# Leave blank to skip.
+TMUX_STATUS_STYLE="${AGENT_TMUX_STATUS_STYLE:-}"
+TMUX_PANE_BORDER_STYLE="${AGENT_TMUX_PANE_BORDER_STYLE:-}"
+TMUX_PANE_ACTIVE_BORDER_STYLE="${AGENT_TMUX_PANE_ACTIVE_BORDER_STYLE:-}"
+CLAUDE_RENAME_TO="${AGENT_CLAUDE_RENAME_TO:-}"
+
 log() { printf '[%s] %s\n' "$(date -Is)" "$*"; }
 
 wait_for_ready() {
@@ -60,13 +70,36 @@ send_loop_prompt() {
   tmux send-keys -t "$SESSION" Enter
 }
 
+apply_tmux_styling() {
+  # Re-apply per-session tmux styling. tmux set is per-session and is lost
+  # when the session is killed, so we run this every start_session.
+  [ -n "$TMUX_STATUS_STYLE" ] && tmux set -t "$SESSION" status-style "$TMUX_STATUS_STYLE" 2>/dev/null || true
+  [ -n "$TMUX_PANE_BORDER_STYLE" ] && tmux set -t "$SESSION" pane-border-style "$TMUX_PANE_BORDER_STYLE" 2>/dev/null || true
+  [ -n "$TMUX_PANE_ACTIVE_BORDER_STYLE" ] && tmux set -t "$SESSION" pane-active-border-style "$TMUX_PANE_ACTIVE_BORDER_STYLE" 2>/dev/null || true
+}
+
+send_claude_rename() {
+  # Claude Code's /rename slash command labels the session footer. Lost on
+  # respawn, so we re-send after each /loop prompt. Must run AFTER the
+  # /loop has been submitted, otherwise /rename gets consumed as part of it.
+  [ -z "$CLAUDE_RENAME_TO" ] && return 0
+  log "sending /rename $CLAUDE_RENAME_TO"
+  tmux send-keys -t "$SESSION" -l "/rename $CLAUDE_RENAME_TO"
+  sleep 1
+  tmux send-keys -t "$SESSION" Enter
+  sleep 1
+}
+
 start_session() {
   log "starting tmux session $SESSION"
   tmux kill-session -t "$SESSION" 2>/dev/null || true
   # Pass AGENT_LAUNCH_CMD expanded, not via a wrapper — see comment above.
   tmux new-session -d -s "$SESSION" -c "$WORKDIR" "$AGENT_LAUNCH_CMD"
+  apply_tmux_styling
   if wait_for_ready; then
     send_loop_prompt
+    sleep 5
+    send_claude_rename
   else
     log "agent TUI did not show ready marker within ${READY_TIMEOUT_SEC}s; will retry on next tick"
   fi

@@ -78,6 +78,22 @@ AGENT_NOTIFY_ON_PHRASE_REGEX=usage limit|out of extra usage|Claude usage|rate li
 AGENT_NOTIFY_ON_PHRASE_TITLE=Agent hit usage limit
 AGENT_NOTIFY_ON_PHRASE_BODY=Session is blocked on a quota. Log in with another account or wait for the reset; the supervisor will NOT change credentials on its own. Work in flight is preserved in whatever ledger the agent uses (see your policy).
 
+# Display persistence across respawns. tmux session styling and Claude Code's
+# /rename label are both per-session and vanish when the session is killed
+# (supervisor respawn / renew timer / healthcheck stall / reboot). Set these
+# to re-apply them every spawn so your session always looks the same when
+# you attach. Leave blank to skip.
+#
+# Common tmux colors: colour22=green, colour24=cyan, colour53=magenta,
+# colour94=tan, colour208=orange, colour22-254 are all 256-color codes.
+AGENT_TMUX_STATUS_STYLE=
+AGENT_TMUX_PANE_BORDER_STYLE=
+AGENT_TMUX_PANE_ACTIVE_BORDER_STYLE=
+
+# The value you want Claude Code to show as its session label (what /rename
+# sets). Leave blank if you don't use Claude Code or don't want it labeled.
+AGENT_CLAUDE_RENAME_TO=
+
 # Seconds of heartbeat silence before healthcheck considers the agent stalled.
 # Default is generous: typical /loop cron cadence is 15 min, scans take 5-10
 # min, so 30 min of silence is real trouble.
